benchmark.name: LongMemEval_S
benchmark.cutoff_note: Cutoffs 20 and 50 were pre-declared before the run to halve answerer input cost; the evaluation_audit strict flag official_harness_score_complete refers to the legacy four-cutoff family (10/20/50/200) and is expected to be false for this record.

official_scores.denominator_policy: all 500 questions retained, including any failed samples

preflight.note: development data (previously inspected calibration set); run before freeze to validate cost and formatting

notes: The Mem0 94.8% reference used GPT-5 as answerer and judge; this run used GLM 5.2 answering with a DeepSeek V4 Flash judge, so accuracy is not a controlled head-to-head.
Latency compares a local NarratorDB deployment against Mem0's managed platform: operational evidence, not a controlled infrastructure comparison.

Versus the 1.2 all-500 record (same judge model family), the answerer changed from V4 Flash to GLM 5.2 and cutoffs narrowed to 20/50; the +4.8pp top-50 gain reflects reader change plus retrieval shaping and cannot be attributed to either alone.
The adapter merges position-adjacent same-session hits into single memory blocks; this is backend response formatting and is documented as part of the tested system.

leakage_control.tuning_data: LoCoMo diagnostic (tests/data/locomo10.json), golden-ordering parity corpus, and full-500 aggregate coverage counts only; no per-question answers, labels, or judge outputs were inspected
leakage_control.dev42_ids_unavailable_note: the dev-42 ID list (gitignored reports/ archive) was not present on the machine used for this cycle; per-scope dev-42 and holdout-458 score views are therefore not part of this record
